data.title: BNB Drops Below 610 USDT with a 0.88% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 17, 2026, 14:46 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 610 USDT and is now trading at 609.830017 USDT, with a narrowed 0.88% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Malaysia Increases Gold Reserves for First Time Since 2018
data.description: Malaysia's central bank has increased its gold reserves by 3 tons in January, marking the first such increase since October 2018. According to Jin10, this brings the country's total gold reserves to 42 tons. The move reflects a strategic decision by the central bank to bolster its gold holdings amid global economic uncertainties. The International Monetary Fund's latest data highlights this development as part of Malaysia's broader financial strategy.

data.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $67,000 Amid Market Activity
data.description: Bitcoin has experienced a decline, dropping below the $67,000 mark. Crypto Rover posted on X that the cryptocurrency fell by $1,000 within 20 minutes of the U.S. market opening. This movement in Bitcoin's value highlights the ongoing volatility in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Equities Stabilize Amid AI Concerns
data.description:
U.S. equities have shown signs of stabilization following a period of anxiety driven by artificial intelligence developments. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the market's recovery after a month marked by uncertainty. The initial apprehension surrounding AI's impact on various sectors had led to fluctuations in stock prices, but recent trends indicate a return to steadiness.

Investors had been closely monitoring AI advancements, which initially sparked concerns about potential disruptions in the market. However, as the month progressed, confidence began to rebuild, contributing to the stabilization of equities.

Market analysts suggest that while AI continues to be a significant factor in investment strategies, the initial panic has subsided, allowing for a more measured approach to stock trading. This shift in sentiment has been reflected in the performance of major indices, which have shown resilience despite earlier volatility.

The stabilization of U.S. equities comes as investors adapt to the evolving landscape shaped by technological innovations. As AI continues to influence market dynamics, stakeholders are expected to remain vigilant, balancing optimism with caution in their investment decisions.

data.title: Office Market Shows Signs of Expansion in Prime Locations
data.description: The office market is experiencing a resurgence, with growth primarily seen in top-tier locations. Bloomberg posted on X that interest is concentrated in the most desirable cities, where high-end properties are attracting attention. This trend highlights a selective recovery, focusing on premium office spaces in key urban areas.

data.title: World Bank President Ajay Banga Defends Multilateralism
data.description: World Bank President Ajay Banga has expressed confidence in the continued relevance of multilateralism. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Banga's perspective amid growing skepticism about the effectiveness of multilateral institutions. Banga argues that multilateralism remains a crucial framework for addressing global challenges, despite increasing doubts from various quarters. His comments come at a time when many are questioning the ability of international organizations to effectively manage global issues. Banga's stance underscores the importance of cooperation and collaboration among nations to tackle complex problems that transcend borders.

data.title: U.S. Federal Debt to Reach $64 Trillion, Investors Eye Bitcoin as Hedge
data.description: The United States is expected to accumulate almost $64 trillion in federal debt over the next ten years, exceeding the debt-to-GDP ratios seen during World War II. According to NS3.AI, the rising term premiums on long-term Treasuries and increasing fiscal uncertainty are leading investors to view Bitcoin as a potential hedge. Analysts are paying close attention to interest rates, the credibility of the dollar, reserve diversification, and the impact of stablecoins to assess Bitcoin's viability as an investment option.

data.title: Senate Race Between Ken Paxton and Jasmine Crockett Could Be Captivating
data.description: A potential Senate race between Ken Paxton and Jasmine Crockett is anticipated to be highly engaging. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the dynamic personalities of both candidates, which could lead to a compelling political showdown. While the contest promises to be entertaining, some observers express reservations about the potential for heightened drama and tension. The race is expected to draw significant attention due to the contrasting styles and approaches of Paxton and Crockett, making it a focal point in the political landscape.

data.title: Anderson Cooper to Depart CBS's '60 Minutes' After Nearly Two Decades
data.description: Anderson Cooper, the renowned 58-year-old American television news anchor, has announced his departure from CBS's news program '60 Minutes.' According to Ming Pao, this marks the end of his nearly 20-year tenure as a contributing correspondent.

data.title: Bitcoin Cash and Ripple Experience Declines, Affecting CoinDesk 20 Index
data.description: Bitcoin Cash (BCH) experienced a 2.2% decline, contributing to a decrease in the CoinDesk 20 index. According to NS3.AI, Ripple (XRP) also saw a reduction of 1.5%, marking it as another underperformer. This update underscores mild negative trends among some prominent cryptocurrencies.

data.title: U.S. Trade Representative Indicates Possible Adjustment to Steel and Aluminum Tariffs
data.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer has suggested that the Trump administration may consider altering its extensive tariffs on steel and aluminum. Bloomberg posted on X that this potential shift comes in response to mounting pressure from business groups and international trading partners. The tariffs, initially imposed to protect domestic industries, have faced criticism for their impact on global trade relations and economic dynamics. Business leaders and foreign governments have been urging the U.S. to reconsider these measures, highlighting their adverse effects on international commerce and cooperation. The administration's openness to revisiting these tariffs could signal a significant change in U.S. trade policy, aiming to balance domestic interests with global economic partnerships. As discussions continue, stakeholders are closely monitoring any developments that may influence trade strategies and economic outcomes.

data.title: Senegal Secures Funding for Eurobond Payments Due in March
data.description: Senegal has successfully obtained the necessary funding to cover its eurobond payments scheduled for March. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the country's proactive approach in managing its financial obligations. This development comes as Senegal continues to navigate the complexities of international finance, ensuring stability in its economic commitments. The move is seen as a positive step in maintaining investor confidence and supporting the nation's fiscal health.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Germany Engages in Talks with U.S. on Rosneft Deutschland License Extension
data.description: Germany is actively engaging in discussions with U.S. authorities regarding the extension of the general license for Rosneft Deutschland, according to a German Ministry of Economic Affairs official. According to Jin10, these talks are described as very close and constructive, focusing on the Foreign Assets Control Office (OFAC) license. The discussions highlight the ongoing geopolitical considerations surrounding energy and foreign asset management.

data.title: White House Supports Digital Asset Market Transparency Act
data.description: The White House has expressed its support for the Digital Asset Market Transparency Act. According to ChainCatcher, this legislation aims to enhance transparency and regulatory measures within the digital asset market.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Surpasses 2,000 USDT with a 1.51% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 17, 2026, 14:07 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 2,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 2,001.050049 USDT, with a narrowed 1.51% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Senator John Cornyn Faces Challenges in Republican Primary
data.description: In his over 20-year tenure as a U.S. Senator from Texas, John Cornyn has consistently navigated his political career without significant electoral challenges. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this year marks a departure from his usual political landscape as he encounters difficulties advancing beyond the Republican primary. Cornyn's campaign is now focused on overcoming these hurdles to secure his position in the upcoming elections.

data.title: Congress Urged to Exempt Data Agency Personnel from New Rule
data.description: A statistical advocacy group has appealed to Congress to exempt data agency personnel from a forthcoming regulation that will reclassify numerous federal employees into 'policy-influencing' roles. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that the rule could impact the objectivity and independence of data agency staff. The advocacy group argues that such a reclassification may undermine the integrity of data-driven decision-making processes within federal agencies. They emphasize the importance of maintaining a clear distinction between data analysis and policy formulation to ensure unbiased and accurate information is available for public and governmental use. The proposed rule has sparked debate among stakeholders, with some fearing it could politicize data agencies and affect their ability to provide impartial insights. As discussions continue, the group remains steadfast in its call for Congress to consider the implications of the rule and protect the neutrality of data agency personnel.

data.title: AI Startup Emergent Achieves $100 Million Revenue Milestone
data.description: AI startup Emergent, which operates in both India and the United States, announced it has achieved a $100 million annual revenue run rate. Bloomberg posted on X that this milestone comes less than a year after the company launched its artificial intelligence-powered software development platform. Emergent's rapid growth highlights the increasing demand for AI-driven solutions in the software development industry.

data.title: The US and Iran reached an agreement on "some overall issues" during the second round of negotiations.
data.description: In the second round of Iran-US talks that concluded on the 17th, the two sides reached an agreement on some general issues. After the negotiating delegations briefed their respective governments, Iran and the US will continue to discuss details. On the 6th of this month, representatives from Iran and the United States held indirect talks in Muscat, the capital of Oman, and reached a consensus on continuing the negotiations, but serious differences remained on core issues. The second round of indirect talks between the US and Iran began on the 17th in Geneva, Switzerland. The talks were held at the Omani diplomatic mission in Geneva. (CCTV News)

data.title: AMC Entertainment Pursues $2.5 Billion Refinancing Plan
data.description: AMC Entertainment is aiming to secure approximately $2.5 billion from credit investors to refinance its current debt obligations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's strategic move to manage its financial liabilities. The theater operator is exploring options to restructure its debt, which could potentially improve its financial stability and operational flexibility. This initiative comes as AMC continues to navigate the challenges posed by the evolving entertainment landscape and fluctuating consumer demand. The refinancing plan is part of AMC's broader strategy to strengthen its balance sheet and ensure long-term sustainability in the competitive market.
